summary: Widen gate/explain's hardcoded skill+rule by-kind corpus to every built-in kind so a memory member's satisfies row reaches roster/graph/coverage instead of being structurally invisible

edit.description: assemble_by_kind (~L896-907) takes exactly `skill_features`/`rule_features` params, not a generic collection. gate()'s built-in loop (~L584-605) captures only `"skill"`/`"rule"` out of its per-kind dispatch via `match kind.name.as_str() { ... _ => {} }`, discarding every other built-in's features (a memory member's declared `satisfies` never reaches `by_kind`/`all_features`, ~L662-671, though gate's own two-greens validate it fine). explain() (~L400-438) independently re-derives the identical shape by hand — fetching `skill_kind`/`rule_kind` by name rather than looping `builtin_kind::definitions()` — a second implementation of the same corpus assembly, so `gate` and `explain` can silently disagree. Consolidate: one shared helper loops every `builtin_defs` kind (as gate's two-greens loop already does) into a generic `BTreeMap<String, Vec<Features>>`; `assemble_by_kind` takes that map plus `custom_kinds`; both `gate()` and `explain()` call the one helper.

tests.asserts: a memory member's satisfies edge counts toward a requirement's cardinality/roster/graph-degree/coverage checks, not only skill/rule members
explain reports a memory member as a requirement's satisfier when the lock's own satisfies row says so, matching gate's verdict

acceptance: explain <requirement> and roster::check/graph::degree/coverage::check agree that a memory member's declared satisfies row counts; gate() and explain() share one corpus-assembly implementation instead of two independent ones.
notes: Field-reported (observed at f9cc899), re-verified live at HEAD: main.rs:582-605 dispatch, :896-907 assemble_by_kind, :400-438 explain's independent re-derivation all confirmed on disk. Sibling BUILTIN_DEFAULT_CONTRACT_KINDS (main.rs:57, schema command's contract listing) is a distinct, already self-commented deferral ("widening it to memory is a separate question") — out of scope here. Pre-existing debt from skill_rule_corpus (c9a576c), not a new regression. scoped at 967b0e6

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at dd38241 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
